title: audit — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Audit registers two hooks: the tenant-erasure MIGRATE-to-archive cascade (audit is DIFFERENT from analytics/marketing/activity — no cascade-delete) and the request-context application_id snapshot on audit creating.

hooks.name: PurgeAuditDataForErasedTenantHook
hooks.purpose: MIGRATE audit rows for the erased tenant to a dedicated S3 archive bucket (`AUDIT_ERASURE_ARCHIVE_S3_BUCKET`). Do NOT cascade-delete — regulators may need proof of the erasure years later. The archive object is: (a) named by erasure event id + tenant id; (b) written with S3 object-lock (compliance mode, indefinite); (c) referenced from an ErasureArchiveManifest table for later regulator discovery. AuditRetentionPolicy IS cascade-deleted (its target tenant is gone; the policy no longer applies).
steps: Load the tenant's audit rows (streaming query, batched 10k rows/batch).
Stream to a jsonl.gz file (one file per tenant, or chunked by month for very large tenants).
Multipart upload to AUDIT_ERASURE_ARCHIVE_S3_BUCKET with object-lock compliance mode.
Write an ErasureArchiveManifest row (bucket, key, row_count, min/max created_at, sha256, tenant_id, erasure_event_id).
DELETE the audit rows from the primary table.
DELETE the tenant's AuditRetentionPolicy row (cascade delete).
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 balanced against Art. 30. Erasing a subject or tenant DOES NOT erase the record of the processing that occurred while they were a subject/tenant. The migration to a compliance archive satisfies BOTH — the tenant's active-processing rows are removed from the primary system, while the regulator retains ability to prove what happened during the tenant's lifecycle.

hooks.name: SnapshotApplicationIdOnAuditCreatingHook
hooks.purpose: Runs BEFORE our AuditObserver::creating. When the vendor observer produces an audit row without an application_id (which is always — the vendor doesn't know about our column), this hook resolves the current request's application_id from the ResolveApplication middleware's binding (`X-Application-Id` header). Populates audit.application_id from that. Skipped for platform-plane audits (no X-Application-Id header on platform routes) — audit.application_id stays NULL.
steps: Read the vendor-produced audit row model instance.
IF audit.application_id IS NOT NULL: skip (already set by caller or downstream hook).

no_bootstrapper.reason: Audit does not need a per-request tenant hydration path — the vendor observer + our AuditObserver handle everything at the model-event boundary. Contrast with modules like `tenancy` (bootstraps request-context) or `settings` (bootstraps config cache) that need per-request tenant hydration.
